title: hieracium vogesiacum subsp. benthamianum
intro: Hieracium vogesiacum subsp. benthamianum, also known as Hawkweed or Hieracium benthamianum, is a plant species within the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Vosges Mountains in France. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. These properties make it beneficial for treating various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive problems. Hieracium vogesiacum subsp. benthamianum can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
